63.reason: [The program uses a hardcoded absolute Windows path, but the problem requires reading from "article.txt" in the current directory. This will cause file not found on other systems or when the file is in the current working directory.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The program uses a hardcoded absolute Windows path, but the problem requires reading from "article.txt" in the current directory. This will cause file not found on other systems or when the file is in the current working directory.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The program uses a hardcoded absolute path instead of the required "article.txt" in the current directory, which will cause file not found on other systems.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The problem specifies the file must be read from "article.txt" in the current directory, but this uses an absolute Windows path. This will fail on systems without this exact path or on non-Windows systems.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem specifies that the input file is named "article.txt" and located in the current directory. Using an absolute path like "D:\\ccode\\..." will fail on other systems or when the file is not at that exact location. It should be just "article.txt".]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The program uses a hardcoded absolute Windows path, but the problem requires reading from "article.txt" in the current directory. This will cause file not found on other systems or when the file is not at that exact path.]

71.reason: [If the file is empty or starts with EOF, this loop may read past EOF without checking feof/ferror, leading to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [If the file is empty or starts with EOF, this loop may read past EOF without checking feof/ferror, leading to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [If the file is empty or contains no alphabetic characters, this loop may read past EOF without checking, leading to undefined behavior. Also, feof() should be checked after fgetc().]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If the file is empty or contains no alphabetic characters, this loop may read past EOF without checking, leading to undefined behavior. Also, if `fgetc` returns EOF immediately, `isalpha(EOF)` is false, so the loop exits, but then the following `while(isalpha(c))` won't run, leaving `dlist` uninitialized properly.]

21.reason: [The condition `strcmp(p->word,r->word)<=0 && p!=NULL` checks `p!=NULL` after dereferencing `p`, which can cause a segmentation fault if `p` is NULL. The correct order should be `p != NULL && strcmp(p->word, r->word) <= 0`.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition `p!=NULL` should be checked before `strcmp(p->word, r->word)` to avoid dereferencing a NULL pointer. The current order may cause a segmentation fault when `p` becomes NULL.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition `strcmp(p->word,r->word)<=0 && p!=NULL` checks `p!=NULL` after dereferencing `p`, which can cause a segmentation fault if `p` is NULL. The correct order should be `p != NULL && strcmp(p->word, r->word) <= 0`.]

98.reason: [feof(IN) only returns true after an attempt to read past EOF. At this point, `c` may already be EOF, and tolower(EOF) is undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [feof(IN) only returns true after an attempt to read past EOF. At this point, `c` may already be EOF, and tolower(EOF) is undefined behavior.]
